Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 1, 037 [Sakikku 16][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.DU-su GU₇-šu₂ U₄.DA.TAB.BA ŠU DINGIR AD-A.NI GAM

(o 1) (If) he is ill for 1 day and his head hurts him: heat stroke; hand of the god of his father; he will die.

o 22

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG-ma ina ŠA₃-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GAR.GARan ši-gu-u₂ GU₃si ŠU-MIN-šu₂ NIR.NIRaṣ GAM

(o 2) (If) he is ill for 1 day and he continually puts his hands on his belly, cries out in pain (and) continually stretches out his hands: he will die.

o 33

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-ma ina ŠA₃-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ GAR.GAR U-MEŠ-šu₂ u₂-na-ṣab ŠU DINGIR rab-bu-ti GAM

(o 3) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he continually puts his hands on his belly (and) sucks his fingers: hand of the great gods; he will die.

o 44

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-ma ana E₂-šu₂ ER₂ GAM

(o 4) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he weeps for his house: he will die.

o 55

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-ma ana DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ ER₂ GAM

(o 5) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he weeps for his children: he will die.

o 66

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-⸢ma⸣ ŠE₁₀ pa₂-ru-ti-šu₂ iz-zi GAM

(o 6) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he ejects excrement in his vomit: he will die.

o 77

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-⸢ma⸣ ina KAŠ₃-šu₂ UŠ₂ iš-tin ana KI GIG GAM

(o 7) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he urinates blood in his urine: he is afflicted by the Underworld; he will die.

o 88

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-ma i-leb-bu u i-sa-aʾ GAM

(o 8) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he howls and cries out in pain: he will die.

o 99

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-ma a-a GU₃.GU₃-ma la₃ NAG ŠU DINGIR GAL.GAL GAM

(o 9) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he continually cries "ah!" and does not drink: hand of the great gods; he will die.

o 1010

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-ma UŠ₂ MUD iz-zi GAM : ana GAL₅.LA₂ UŠ₂ pa-qid GAM

(o 10) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and he defecates dark blood: he will die : (variant:) he is entrusted to the demon of death; he will die.

o 1111

* KI.MIN <(U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG)>-ma GIG-su DUGUD-su-ma ZU-šu₂ la₃ u₂-ad₂-da GAM

(o 11) Ditto ((If) he is ill for 1 day) and his illness becomes burdensome and he no longer recognizes his acquaintance: he will die.

o 1212

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ TAG₄-šu₂-ma U₄ 1-KAM LAL-šu₂ NIG₂.GU₇ dDIM₂.ME.LAGAB ŠU DINGIR GAL-MEŠ GAM

(o 12) (If the illness) leaves him for 1 day and overwhelms him (again) for 1 day: food for the seizer-demon; hand of the great gods; he will die.

o 1313

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ DAB-su U₄ 1-KAM₂ BAR-šu₂ ZI DINGIR DAB-su

(o 13) (If the illness) afflicts him for 1 day, leaves him for 1 day: an attack of the god seized him.

o 1414

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ DAB-su U₄ 1-KAM₂ BAR-šu₂ U₄ LA₂-šu₂ LA₂-šu₂ UB-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ KUM₂-ma i-ra-ʾu-ub

(o 14) (If the illness) afflicts him for 1 day, leaves him for 1 day, (and) when his fit overwhelms him (again), his limbs hurt him, he is feverish and trembles, he sweats and is tired and continually strongly requests to drink water: hand of Lamaštu; he will recover.

o 1515

IR ŠUB-su-ma ina-aḫ u A ana NAG ma-gal URU₄-MEŠ ŠU dDIM₂.ME ⸢DIN⸣

o 1616

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ GIG U₄ 1-KAM₂ ba-liṭ GIDIM AD u AMA DAB-[su]

(o 16) (If) he is ill for 1 day (and) is healthy for 1 day: the ghost of father and mother seized him.

o 1717

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ U₄ 2-KAM₂ i-re-[eḫ-ḫi]-⸢šum⸣-ma ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ DAB-su DABit [...]

(o 17) (If the illness) flows over him for 1 day, 2 days and seizes him the third day: seizure of [...].

o 1818

* U₄ 1-KAM₂ U₄ 2-KAM₂ U₄ 3-[KAM₂] ⸢GIG⸣-ma KUM₂ UḪ₂ ina SU-šu₂ DU₈-ma ⸢EGIR⸣ [...]

(o 18) (If) he is ill for 1 day, 2 days, 3 [days] and then the burning fever lets up from his body and after [...] the burning fever does not let up from his head: [...] to 3 days or 6 days.

o 1919

KUM₂ UḪ₂ ina SAG.⸢DU⸣-[šu₂ la₃] DU₈ ana U₄ 3-KAM₂ ana U₄ 6-KAM₂ [...]

o 2020

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.KI-⸢šu₂⸣ [ša₂ 15] ⸢GU₇⸣-šu₂ IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 GISSU DU₃ ŠU dU₄.AL.TAR BUR₂-[ma ...]

(o 20) (If) he is ill for 2 days and his right temple hurts him, his right eye forms a dark spot: the hand of Jupiter will be released [...].

o 2121

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ ⸢2⸣.[30] ⸢GU₇⸣-šu₂ IGI-šu₂ ša₂ 2.30 GISSU DU₃ ŠU dU₄.AL.TAR BUR₂-⸢ma⸣ [...]

(o 21) (If) he is ill for 2 days and his left temple hurts him, his left eye forms a dark spot: the hand of Jupiter will be released and [...].

o 2222

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ GIG-ma SAG.KI-šu₂ ša₂ 15 u ⸢2⸣.30 GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI-MIN-šu₂ ša₂ 15 u 2.30 GISSU ib-[na-a]

(o 22) (If) he is ill for 2 days and his right and left temples hurt him, his right and left eyes form a dark spot: this person, his god or the god of his town have cursed (him); they will be released and [...].

o 2323

NA BI DINGIR-šu₂ u DINGIR IRI-šu₂ iz-zur BUR₂-MEŠ-⸢ma⸣ [...]

o 2424

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ ŠUB-šu₂-ma ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ DAB-su U₄ma DAB-šu₂ im-ta-na-ag-ga-gu EGIR im-ta-⸢nag?⸣-[ga?-ag?]

(o 24) (If the illness) pours over him for 2 days and then seizes him on the third day, when it has seized him he has repeated cramps (and) after he has repeated cramps, he is affected by shivering, his limbs hurt him, his hands and feet are cold (and) afterwards [the fever] altogether mounts in him : (variant:) pours over him, and the sweat strikes him and he is tired: seizure of the mountain [...].

o 2525

ra-i-ba TUKUši UB-MEŠ-šu₂ GU₇-MEŠ-šu₂ ŠU-MIN-šu₂ u GIR₃-MIN-šu₂ SEDa EGIR[nu KUM₂]

o 2626

1niš i-rak-kab-šu₂ : i-re-eḫ-ḫi-šu₂ u IR ŠUB-su-ma ina-aḫ DAB KURi [...]

o 2727

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ DAB-su ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ i-re-eḫ-ḫi-šu₂ DABit KURi : GAM

(o 27) (If the illness) afflicts him for 2 days (and) pours over him on the third day: seizure of the mountain : he will die.

o 2828

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ U₄ 3-KAM₂ GIG-ma uš-tar-di-ma ŠA₃-šu₂ DAB.DAB-su UŠ₂ ina KA-šu₂ ŠUB.⸢ŠUB⸣a ⸢GIG-su?⸣ [...]

(o 28) (If) he is ill for 2 days, 3 days and then it persists so that his intestines continually seize him, blood continually flows in his mouth, his illness [...].

o 2929

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ U₄ 3-KAM₂ GIG-ma uš-tar-di-ma ḫu-qu DAB-su ina U₄ 7-KAM₂ ina? [...]

(o 29) (If) he is ill for 2 days, 3 days and then it persists so that the (critical) stage (of the illness) seizes him, on the seventh day in [...].

o 3030

* U₄ 2-KAM₂ U₄ 3-KAM₂ GIG-ma KUM₂ ṣar-ḫu ina SU-šu₂ DU₈-ma EGIR DU₈ ina [...]; ⸢GIG-su⸣

(o 30) (If) he is ill for 2 days, 3 days and then the burning fever lets up from his body (and) after (the fever) has reduced on [...] his illness [...].

o 3131

[...] ina U₄ 2-KAM₂ ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ KUM₂ SED u IR-su GIM lu-ba-⸢ṭi⸣ [...]

(o 31) [...] (If) on the second (and) third days the fever becomes cold and his sweat like the lubādu-disease [...].

o 3232

[... ina U₄] 2-KAM₂ ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ ša₂ DAB ŠUB.ŠUB-su KUM₂ u IR TUKU [...]

(o 32) [... (If) on] the second (and) third days that-of-seizing has continually stricken him, he has fever and sweat [...].

o 3333

[... ina U₄ 2]-⸢KAM₂⸣ ina U₄ 3-KAM₂ [...] ⸢KUM₂?⸣ u IR TUKU x [...]

(o 33) [... (If) on the second] (and) third days [...], he has fever and sweat ... [...].

(end obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (missing)
